title: Lust howls
description: A 5-year arc of 'lust howls' / 'howl' / 'calls' phrases recurring across alphabets-era track titles, 2007–2013.
body:
# Lust howls

The phrase "lust howls" — and its sibling "lust calls" — appears across a five-year arc of alphabets-era recordings. First spotted in a curator filename sweep on 2026-04-24.

## Confirmed appearances

- `07_08 [_]/lust howls in the afternoon.mp3` (2007–2008)
- `07_08 [_]/lust howls and calls for mating like locusts do.m4a` (2007–2008)
- `09 [_]/lust calls and sex dance.m4a` (related "lust calls" variant, 2009)
- `unreleased/lust howls.mp3` (2013, [[releases/unreleased-loose]])

The 2013 track is the latest in the arc. The `.asd` Ableton sidecar on the 2013 file means Colin had it loaded as late as September 2015 — possibly reworking it, possibly auditioning.

## Related motif: "howl"

The broader "howl" theme runs through the catalog independently:

- "robo goats howl and bang" (fantazia2010)
- "plesiosaur howl outs and swim bump" (BESTUV09 + sep09)
- "robodogs nighttime cage howls" (DEC09SCIFI)

Whether these belong to the same motif as "lust howls" or constitute a separate "howl" motif is a curator call.

## Context

The recurrence isn't a one-off; it's a thread Colin returned to. Like the underwaters series's aquatic-creature naming, the howl/lust phrasing is one of his stylistic signatures. Worth treating as a motif/series rather than letting it sit buried in track titles.
